name: System Prompt: Harness instructions
description: Core interactive-agent identity and harness instructions for terminal markdown output, permissions, system reminders, compaction, tool use, and code references
audit_details: Instructs the agent on basic terminal environment details (markdown format), permissions mode handling (adjust when denied), handling system-reminder tags, tool usage preferences, and linking files/lines.
gemini_rationale: Google Antigravity SDK has similar terminal environments and permission systems, but references and system reminders are handled natively through SDK protocols rather than specific <system-reminder> HTML tags. Tool options and links also follow similar conventions.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the harness guidelines. Adapt the <system-reminder> tag concept to Antigravity's internal hook and event notifications. Adapt permission failure adjustments to handle Antigravity's interactive policy responses, and map file:line links to workspace paths.

name: System Prompt: Hooks Configuration
description: System prompt for hooks configuration.  Used for above Claude Code config skill.
audit_details: Details Claude Code hooks schema including PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, and PreCompact, Command/Prompt/Agent hook types, and hook outputs to control execution.
gemini_rationale: Antigravity SDK does not use JSON hooks. It uses standard Python event handlers (e.g. subclassing SDK base hooks or registering callbacks).
gemini_strategy: Bypass the JSON hook schema. Use Python classes or functions to register hooks in LocalAgentConfig. Show developers how to hook into pre_turn, post_turn, pre_tool_call, etc. in Python.

name: System Prompt: How to use the SendUserMessage tool
description: Instructions for using the SendUserMessage tool
audit_details: Directs the agent to send user-facing replies only through the SendUserMessage tool, handle progress notifications using acks, and keep updates concise.
gemini_rationale: Antigravity does not have a SendUserMessage tool for talking to the user. Instead, any text output outside of tool calls is streamed to the user. Using a dummy SendUserMessage tool would cause errors.
gemini_strategy: Bypass the SendUserMessage tool instructions. Instruct the agent to output user-facing communications directly as plain text/markdown responses in the normal conversation stream, while reserving send_message strictly for inter-agent communication.

name: System Prompt: Insights at a glance summary
description: Generates a concise 4-part summary (what's working, hindrances, quick wins, ambitious workflows) for the insights report
audit_details: Specifies a JSON schema for summarizing user sessions into 'What's working', 'What's hindering you', 'Quick wins to try', and 'Ambitious workflows'.
gemini_rationale: Session summarization is a generic task and can be useful for developers using Gemini. However, the suggestions and features reference (e.g. MCP, CLAUDE.md) must be adapted to Gemini equivalents.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the JSON summarization schema. Adapt the recommended features to point to Google Antigravity features (e.g. GEMINI.md, Python lifecycle hooks, parallel tool calling).

name: System Prompt: Insights friction analysis
description: Analyzes aggregated usage data to identify friction patterns and categorize recurring issues
audit_details: Prompts the agent to identify 3 friction categories with 2 examples each based on user logs and return a JSON structure.
gemini_rationale: Identifying user friction from transcripts is a model-agnostat feature. The schema and guidelines are directly reusable, but we adapt the examples to catch Gemini/Antigravity integration errors.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the friction analysis schema. Map typical issues to Gemini-specific topics like API rate limits (429), model configuration errors, or unrecognized tool schemas in LocalAgentConfig.

name: System Prompt: Insights on the horizon
description: Identifies ambitious future workflows and opportunities for autonomous AI-assisted development
audit_details: Instructs the agent to identify 3 future opportunities for autonomous workflows, parallel agents, or test-driven iterations, outputting JSON.
gemini_rationale: Analyzing session data to suggest future agentic workflows is fully compatible with Gemini.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the opportunities JSON structure. Guide the opportunities toward leveraging Antigravity's native features such as multi-agent collaboration, structured JSON schemas, and context caching.

name: System Prompt: Insights session facets extraction
description: Extracts structured facets (goal categories, satisfaction, friction) from a single Claude Code session transcript
audit_details: Guides the agent to count goals, user satisfaction signals, and friction types (misunderstood, wrong approach, buggy code) strictly from explicit signals.
gemini_rationale: This is a telemetry-processing prompt that is generic to conversational agents.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the session facet extraction rules. The extracted metrics can be saved in local telemetry JSON files in the app_data_dir for later analysis.

name: System Prompt: Insights suggestions
description: Generates actionable suggestions including CLAUDE.md additions, features to try, and usage patterns
audit_details: Outlines how to generate suggestions using a JSON schema, including CLAUDE.md changes and Claude features (MCP, Skills, Hooks, Headless, Task Agents).
gemini_rationale: The Claude-specific features referenced (MCP servers, Skills, Hooks) must be replaced with their Google Antigravity SDK equivalents.
gemini_strategy: Adapt the suggestions prompt. Replace CLAUDE.md recommendations with GEMINI.md recommendations. Map Claude features to Antigravity alternatives: MCP config, Python-based custom skills, lifecycle hooks, and subagent delegation.

name: System Prompt: Learning mode (insights)
description: Instructions for providing educational insights when learning mode is active
audit_details: Instructs the agent to present brief educational explanations of implementation choices using a structured box format with stars and borders.
gemini_rationale: The formatted box layout and coaching style is completely compatible with Gemini and improves user experience.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the formatting style verbatim. Use it to print insights on code edits when learning mode is enabled in the workspace.

name: System Prompt: Learning mode
description: Main system prompt for learning mode with human collaboration instructions
audit_details: Guides the agent to request user code contributions for design decisions, business logic, or algorithms, adding TODO(human) comment and pausing the turn.
gemini_rationale: Encouraging human collaboration is a powerful pattern. However, the task lists and tools used to add TODOs must align with Antigravity's file tools.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the collaboration workflow. Use the SDK's edit_file or replace_file_content to insert TODO(human) comments. Ensure that when a contribution is requested, the agent stops calling tools and asks the user, resuming once the user responds.

name: System Prompt: Memory description of user details
description: Describes the purpose and guidelines for per-user memory files that accumulate details about the user's role, goals, knowledge, and preferences across sessions
audit_details: Describes the purpose of storing user details (role, preferences, goals) to customize interaction styles across sessions.
gemini_rationale: Storing user context helps personalize interactions. In Antigravity, we store this in local config files or memory stores.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the user profile memory description. Store user details under ~/.gemini/antigravity/memory/user_profile.json or as individual memory files, using them to guide agent tone and technical level.

name: System Prompt: Memory description of user feedback (with explicit save)
description: Describes the feedback memory type that captures user guidance on work approaches, emphasizing recording both successes and failures and explicitly instructing to save a new memory noting contradictions with team feedback
audit_details: Guides storing user feedback (successes/failures) and resolving contradictions with team memories by saving overriding private records.
gemini_rationale: Keeping track of user feedback is key to avoiding repeated mistakes.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the feedback memory description. Implement checking against team guidelines (GEMINI.md or global team config) before writing personal overrides.

name: System Prompt: Memory description of user feedback
description: Describes the user feedback memory type that stores guidance about work approaches, emphasizing recording both successes and failures and checking for contradictions with team memories
audit_details: Details recording feedback from both success and failure and checking against team memory files.
gemini_rationale: Identical context to the explicit save version, defining user feedback storage rules.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the guidelines. Personal feedback memories are written to the user's local memory store and merged appropriately.

name: System Prompt: Memory staleness verification
description: Instructs the agent to verify memory records against current file/resource state and delete stale memories that conflict with observed reality
audit_details: Advises verifying recalled memories against actual file/resource state before acting, and deleting/updating stale memories.
gemini_rationale: Preventing memory drift is critical. Gemini models should verify facts rather than trusting older memory files blindly.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the verification guidelines. When a memory conflicts with the current file contents, delete or overwrite the stale memory file in the memory directory.

name: System Prompt: Minimal mode
description: Describes the behavior and constraints of minimal mode, which skips hooks, LSP, plugins, auto-memory, and other features while requiring explicit context via CLI flags
audit_details: Outlines the minimal mode constraints (disabling auto-memory, hooks, LSP, keychain) and CLI flags to supply manual context.
gemini_rationale: Antigravity is programmatically configured. We don't need CLI overrides for settings; instead, we pass LocalAgentConfig(capabilities=CapabilitiesConfig(enable_subagents=False, enable_commands=False)) to start a minimal sandbox.
gemini_strategy: Adapt the minimal mode concept to programmatic configuration. Provide a minimal_config template that disables external tools and integrations, ensuring high-speed single-turn operation.

name: System Prompt: One of six rules for using sleep command
description: One of the six rules for using the sleep command.
audit_details: A single line warning against using a sleep loop to retry failing commands, advising the agent to diagnose the root cause instead.
gemini_rationale: Running commands in loops wastes system resources, billing tokens, and time. In Google Antigravity, we want agents to fail fast, report the failure details, and self-correct using log diagnostics instead of spinning.
gemini_strategy: Adopt this rule. Instruct the model to immediately inspect files, run tests, or check environment configs when a command fails rather than executing it again with a delay.

name: System Prompt: Option previewer
description: System prompt for previewing UI options in a side-by-side layout
audit_details: Describes how to use the optional preview field for interactive single-select questions to display ASCII layout mockups, code snippets, or configuration examples in a side-by-side split screen panel.
gemini_rationale: Interactive multi-choice questions are supported by the Antigravity SDK via ask_question. Supporting visual previews in a side-by-side terminal or web interface is fully compatible and improves usability.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the preview guidelines. Use the preview field format within structured question outputs, allowing the terminal or web wrapper of the Antigravity CLI to render comparison views.

name: System Prompt: Phase four of plan mode
description: Phase four of plan mode.
audit_details: Instructs the agent on how to write the final plan to a plan file (e.g. plan.md), covering problem context, specific files to edit, patterns, reusable code, and e2e verification steps.
gemini_rationale: Having a designated plan file is a best practice for complex agentic workflows. In Antigravity, we use task.md or plan.md in the workspace directory.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the plan mode Phase 4 instructions. Instruct the agent to write a structured plan.md file using create_file or write_to_file in the local workspace directory, containing Context, Approach, Affected Files, and Verification.

name: System Prompt: PowerShell edition for 5.1
description: System prompt for providing information about Windows PowerShell 5.1
audit_details: Identifies syntactic limitations of PowerShell 5.1 on Windows (no pipeline chaining, no ternary operators, native command error record redirect bugs, default UTF-16 encoding, and PSCustomObject return types) and provides workarounds.
gemini_rationale: Developers on Windows environments will use PowerShell. Since Gemini writes scripts and commands executed by the terminal, understanding these shell quirks prevents syntax and execution crashes.
gemini_strategy: Adopt PowerShell 5.1 safety rules. When target systems run Windows/PowerShell, ensure the agent generates compatible cmdlet pipes, uses -Encoding utf8, and avoids bash-specific syntax like && or 2>&1.

name: System Prompt: Remote plan mode (ultraplan)
description: System reminder injected during remote planning sessions that instructs Claude to explore the codebase, produce a diagram-rich plan via ExitPlanMode, and implement it with a pull request upon approval
audit_details: Configures remote plan mode behavior where Claude explores code, calls ExitPlanMode with a prose/Mermaid plan, handles approved PR setups, and checks for teleport identifiers to local terminals.
gemini_rationale: Remote planning and local terminal teleportation are features specific to Claude Code's cloud-to-local bridge infrastructure. In Google Antigravity, agents run directly on the user's workspace (local or remote SSH) inside sandboxed containers or local directories, so there is no local-vs-remote handoff or teleportation logic needed.
gemini_strategy: Bypass this system prompt. General planning is handled via local task files and interactive user prompts rather than an external teleport system.

name: System Prompt: Remote planning session
description: System reminder that configures a remote planning session to explore the codebase, produce an implementation plan via ExitPlanMode, and handle plan approval, rejection, or teleportation back to the user's local terminal
audit_details: Directs remote session exploration, calling ExitPlanMode, PR creation on success, local teleport checks, and error responses.
gemini_rationale: Proprietary remote-to-local bridge logic which does not apply to Google Antigravity SDK environments.
gemini_strategy: Bypass. All planning operations are local to the run context, making remote planning modes and plan handoffs redundant.

name: System Prompt: REPL tool usage and scripting conventions
description: Instructs Claude on how to use the REPL tool effectively with dense JavaScript scripts, shorthands, batching rules, and API reference for investigation tasks
audit_details: Details scripting patterns for the JS REPL tool, using dense single-letter variables, shorthands like sh, cat, rg, rgf, gl, put, chdir, and helper APIs for concurrent execution.
gemini_rationale: Google Antigravity SDK does not use a JS VM REPL tool. All local operations use standard, typed Python methods or system commands. Exposing JS REPL conventions would cause the model to generate incorrect JavaScript blocks.
gemini_strategy: Bypass this reference. Replace it with guidelines on python tool calls (e.g. view_file, grep_search, write_to_file) and parallelizing multiple calls within a single model response.

name: System Prompt: Subagent delegation examples
description: Provides example interactions showing how a coordinator agent should delegate tasks to subagents, handle waiting states, and report results
audit_details: Illustrates delegation using agent tool calls, showing how the coordinator handles intermediate user queries during execution, and launches independent reviewers.
gemini_rationale: The subagent architecture in Google Antigravity SDK is highly similar. Spawning subagents is a core multi-agent orchestration pattern. We can adapt these examples to demonstrate the SDK's invoke_subagent and send_message tools.
gemini_strategy: Adopt and adapt. Update the tool calls in the examples to target invoke_subagent with the correct properties (TypeName, Role, Prompt) and show how updates are received via messaging.

name: System Prompt: Subagent prompt-writing examples
description: Provides example usage patterns demonstrating how to write self-contained, well-structured prompts when delegating tasks to subagents
audit_details: Provides examples showing how to write self-contained prompts for subagents, giving enough context for independent judgment and avoiding 'never delegate understanding' pitfalls.
gemini_rationale: The prompt-writing principles for subagents (smart-colleague briefing, explicit goals/constraints, not delegating synthesis) are critical to ensure that subagents return useful, parsing-friendly results.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the examples. Use them to guide the coordinator model's prompt generation when delegating tasks using the invoke_subagent tool.

name: System Prompt: Tone and style (code references)
description: Instruction to include file_path:line_number when referencing code
audit_details: Instructs the agent to always include the file_path:line_number pattern when referencing source code files to enable clickable IDE links.
gemini_rationale: This is a key usability guideline for coding assistants. The Google Antigravity SDK and IDE wrappers parse standard file_path:line_number or file link syntaxes to create click targets.
gemini_strategy: Adopt this rule. Format all code mentions using absolute or relative paths with line numbers (e.g. src/main.py:12-25) as clickable markdown links.

name: System Prompt: Tone and style (concise output — short)
description: Instruction for short and concise responses
audit_details: A single line instructing the agent to keep its responses short and concise.
gemini_rationale: Short responses save tokens, improve speed, and respect the developer's attention span.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. Incorporate this instruction into the default agent configuration, encouraging direct answers and minimizing conversational preamble.

name: System Prompt: Tool usage (subagent guidance)
description: Guidance on when and how to use subagents effectively
audit_details: Outlines the guidelines for when to spawn subagents, explaining they are useful for parallel tasks or context protection, and warning against duplicating searches.
gemini_rationale: The Google Antigravity SDK fully supports subagents. Understanding when to spawn a subagent vs. doing it locally is important for token efficiency and runtime control.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the guidance. Update references from the Claude agent tool to the Antigravity invoke_subagent tool. Use subagents for complex research, and keep the main loop clean.

name: System Prompt: Tool usage (task management)
description: Use TodoWrite to break down and track work progress
audit_details: Recommends breaking down work using the TodoWrite tool and marking tasks as completed immediately without batching.
gemini_rationale: Keeping a task checklist is a highly effective design pattern. While TodoWrite is a specific tool in Claude Code, in Antigravity we can use local files like task.md or a built-in task manager.
gemini_strategy: Adapt to the Antigravity environment. Map TodoWrite instructions to maintaining a structured task.md file in the workspace directory. Mark items completed before moving to the next task.

name: System Prompt: Worker instructions
description: Instructions for workers to follow when implementing a change
audit_details: Provides worker subagents with a five-step post-implementation checklist: run code-review, run tests, test end-to-end, commit/push changes, and report the PR URL or reason for failure.
gemini_rationale: This structural verification workflow ensures high-quality and verified pull requests.
gemini_strategy: Adopt and adapt. Update the 'code-review' skill search to point to the Antigravity skill directory. Ensure testing steps use the local python run command, and commit/PR workflows target standard git actions.

name: System Prompt: Writing subagent prompts
description: Guidelines for writing effective prompts when delegating tasks to subagents, covering context-inheriting vs fresh subagent scenarios
audit_details: Instructs how to write self-contained subagent prompts. Stresses providing enough background context, file paths, line numbers, and forbids delegating synthesis ('based on your findings, fix the bug').
gemini_rationale: Poorly specified prompts for subagents lead to poor quality and model loop drift. Since Antigravity supports both inheriting and fresh subagents, these rules are crucial.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the guidelines. Ensure all subagent invocations include explicit background context, exact targets (files/lines), and specific tasks, rather than generic research commands.

name: System Prompt: WSL managed settings double opt-in
description: Explains that WSL can read the Windows managed settings policy chain only when the admin-enabled flag is set, with HKCU requiring an additional user opt-in
audit_details: Explains the mechanism for reading Windows managed policies under WSL, requiring admin registry flags (HKLM) and HKCU double opt-in validation.
gemini_rationale: This is highly specific to the Claude Code enterprise registry policy implementation on Windows and WSL. The Google Antigravity SDK is configured locally via environment variables and project-level configs rather than checking Windows HKLM/HKCU registry paths, making this policy chain irrelevant.
gemini_strategy: Bypass this system prompt. Manage enterprise settings and agent restrictions using environment variables or a YAML/JSON configuration file checked in or placed in standard config paths (e.g. /etc/antigravity/ or ~/.config/).

name: System Reminder: Agent mention
description: Notification that user wants to invoke an agent
audit_details: Injects a system reminder when the user mentions an agent type, telling the coordinator to invoke that agent with required context.
gemini_rationale: Spawning subagents based on user mentions is fully supported by the Google Antigravity SDK. However, the schema maps to the invoke_subagent tool instead of Claude's agent runner.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the trigger mechanism. Translate agent mentions to invoking subagents using the SDK's invoke_subagent tool with the appropriate role and prompt.

name: System Reminder: Exited plan mode
description: Notification when exiting plan mode
audit_details: Tells the model that plan mode is over, allowing it to edit files, run tools, and make changes.
gemini_rationale: Google Antigravity has a similar concept where after a planning phase, the model transitions to execution.
gemini_strategy: Adopt this lifecycle notification. Use it to trigger transition in the model's behavior from purely readonly analysis to active file editing and command execution.

name: System Reminder: File exists but empty
description: Warning when reading an empty file
audit_details: Injects a warning if the model reads a file that has no content.
gemini_rationale: This is a generic file system boundary check that prevents the model from assuming file read errors.
gemini_strategy: Adopt verbatim. If a view_file call targets an empty file, return this exact warning message in the tool response.

name: System Reminder: File modification detected (budget exceeded)
description: System reminder for when a file modification is detected - specifically when other modified files in the turn already exceeded the budget.
audit_details: Informs the model about an external file modification (user or linter), omitting the diff due to turn budget constraints, and advises using the Read tool if needed.
gemini_rationale: In the Antigravity SDK, we want to warn the model about file changes that occurred between turns without overflowing the context window.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the notification. When multiple files change externally and exceed a designated snippet length, append this notice, directing the model to call view_file to fetch fresh content.

name: System Reminder: File modified by user or linter
description: Notification that a file was modified externally
audit_details: Provides the filename and diff snippet of a file modified by a user or external formatter, warning the model not to revert it.
gemini_rationale: Keeping the agent aware of external edits is crucial to prevent overwrite conflicts.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. Inject this reminder snippet when file updates are detected on disk before a new conversation turn begins, ensuring the model's memory aligns with the filesystem.

name: System Reminder: File opened in IDE
description: Notification that user opened a file in IDE
audit_details: Informs the agent which file the user recently opened in the editor, noting it may be contextually relevant.
gemini_rationale: Tracking IDE file open events helps local developer agents stay aligned with the user's attention.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. Send this file open notification as a system message if the IDE plugin/hook detects the developer changed active editor tabs.

name: System Reminder: File shorter than offset
description: Warning when file read offset exceeds file length
audit_details: Warning message returned when reading a file with an offset range that goes beyond the actual line count of the file.
gemini_rationale: Standard error message to keep tool calls robust and prevent parsing failures.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. Have the view_file tool return this structured warning when the StartLine parameter exceeds the file's total lines.

name: System Reminder: File truncated
description: Notification that file was truncated due to size
audit_details: Tells the model that the requested file was truncated to a maximum line count, warning it not to mention truncation to the user and to use the Read tool for subsequent lines.
gemini_rationale: Context window management is vital. Truncation messages help the model know it has incomplete information.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. When the view_file tool limits output to 800 lines (due to size constraints), append this warning to the tool response so the model knows it needs to request subsequent line offsets if necessary.

name: System Reminder: Hook additional context
description: Additional context from a hook
audit_details: System template used to prefix and append additional context strings generated by executed lifecycle hooks.
gemini_rationale: Programmatic hooks in Google Antigravity SDK can return context strings to steer the model.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the context injection format. When a registered python lifecycle hook (e.g. pre_turn) returns context text, append it as a system reminder in the model's message queue.

name: System Reminder: Hook blocking error
description: Error from a blocking hook command
audit_details: Displays hook name and blocking command error messages to the model when a pre-execution hook fails.
gemini_rationale: Error handling for programmatic hook execution is necessary.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. If a Python hook execution raises an exception, catch it, log it, and inject this error alert to guide model recovery.

name: System Reminder: Hook stopped continuation prefix
description: Prefix for hook stopped continuation messages
audit_details: Simple prefix used to label hook stopped messages in the system reminder context.
gemini_rationale: Clean labeling of hook-interrupted states is helpful.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. Prefix hook-aborted responses with this label before outputting them to the agent's turn results.

name: System Reminder: Hook stopped continuation
description: Message when a hook stops continuation
audit_details: Injects a system warning indicating that a specific hook has halted the agentic execution flow, along with the reason.
gemini_rationale: Lifecycle hooks must be able to cancel agent execution (e.g. static check errors, safety violations).
gemini_strategy: Adopt. If a registered hook cancels the turn, populate this reminder block and halt tool call loops.

name: System Reminder: Hook success
description: Success message from a hook
audit_details: Standard formatting used to report success outputs from hook commands back to the model.
gemini_rationale: Knowing hook outcomes is important for intermediate checks.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. Pass successful hook outputs back to the model using this format to confirm formatting or test passes.

name: System Reminder: Lines selected in IDE
description: Notification about lines selected by user in IDE
audit_details: Informs the agent which exact lines (start/end) from which file the user highlighted in their IDE, displaying the snippet.
gemini_rationale: Highlighting selections helps the agent prioritize specific blocks of code during debugging.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. Integrate with IDE editor highlight event hooks, sending this context snippet to focus the model's attention.

name: System Reminder: MCP resource no content
description: Shown when MCP resource has no content
audit_details: Returns a structured <mcp-resource> tag containing '(No content)' when the agent requests an empty resource.
gemini_rationale: Google Antigravity supports MCP-style integrations. Representing empty resource returns in a clean structured format prevents the model from hallucinating content.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. Translate the template to target standard Antigravity resources, displaying a clear '(No content)' status within resource output blocks.

name: System Reminder: MCP resource no displayable content
description: Shown when MCP resource has no displayable content
audit_details: Formats the resource output as <mcp-resource ...>(No displayable content)</mcp-resource> when content cannot be rendered in text/markdown.
gemini_rationale: Similar to the empty resource template, this handles binary or unsupported formats.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. Use this standard placeholder block when returning resources containing binary data that cannot be formatted as text/markdown to the model.

name: System Reminder: New diagnostics detected
description: Notification about new diagnostic issues
audit_details: Formats diagnostic/linter issue summaries into a <new-diagnostics> block when build or static analysis errors are found on workspace files.
gemini_rationale: Automatically feeding compilation/lint diagnostics back to coding agents helps correct bugs before ending turns.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. Inject this template block populated with linter output (e.g. pyright, eslint, or custom IDE lints) when a command or file save triggers new workspace diagnostics.

name: System Reminder: Output style active
description: Notification that an output style is active
audit_details: Alerts the model about active output styles (e.g. learning mode, concise mode) and prints a turn reminder.
gemini_rationale: Antigravity agents can be configured with style/personality overrides (via system instructions or dynamic prompts).
gemini_strategy: Adopt. When specific user configuration flags are enabled, append this turn-reminder to guide response styling.

name: System Reminder: Plan mode is active (subagent)
description: Simplified plan mode system reminder for sub agents
audit_details: Simplifies planning constraints for subagents, restricting them to read-only tools and incremental plan file updates, and advising them to ask clarifying questions.
gemini_rationale: Subagent planning behaves similarly to coordinator planning but is scaled down to focus on delegatable subtasks.
gemini_strategy: Adopt and adapt. Replace references to Claude's ask-user-question tool with the Antigravity ask_question tool. Retain the read-only safety rules when a subagent is initialized in a planning workspace.

name: System Reminder: Previously invoked skills
description: Restores skills invoked before conversation compaction as context only, warning not to re-execute their setup actions or treat prior inputs as current instructions
audit_details: Appends previously executed skill context after conversation compaction, warning the model not to repeat setup steps and highlighting that inputs are archival.
gemini_rationale: Conversation compaction (truncating old history or tools) is crucial to control context size for long-running sessions. Restoring behavioral guidelines while marking inputs as read-only history prevents model looping.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. When the Antigravity SDK performs turn pruning/compaction, use this header template to re-inject active skill guides as context-only system messages.

name: System Reminder: Session continuation
description: Notification that session continues from another machine
audit_details: Informs the model that the session is resuming on a different machine, updating it with the new current working directory (CWD).
gemini_rationale: Cross-machine session resume is a feature of CLI/cloud sync. In Antigravity, if a stateful agent session is serialized and loaded in a different workspace path or machine, it needs to be aware of the path change.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. When loading a saved agent session from a workspace database onto a new workspace location, inject this path update notification.

name: System Reminder: Stop hook blocking error
description: Error from a blocking hook command
audit_details: Tells the model that a stop hook (executed when finishing a turn or execution) encountered a blocking command error.
gemini_rationale: Part of execution lifecycle error handling.
gemini_strategy: Adopt. If the agent's turn shutdown hook triggers an exception, return this warning message to allow the agent to correct the issue before termination.

name: System Reminder: Task tools reminder
description: Reminder to use task tracking tools
audit_details: Injects a gentle reminder recommending task tool usage (creating/updating task status) if they haven't been invoked in a while.
gemini_rationale: Model guidance to encourage structured task updates. In Antigravity, we manage this by encouraging update edits to a central task.md or using the SDK background task manager.
gemini_strategy: Adapt the reminder. Point the model to update the project task file (task.md) or use the task management tools (manage_task) to reflect actual status.

name: System Reminder: Team Coordination
description: System reminder for team coordination
audit_details: Informs the agent of its identity, team name, team config/task list paths, and team leader name. Details JSON-based messaging formatting using teammate names.
gemini_rationale: Multi-agent collaboration in the Google Antigravity SDK uses named subagents communicating via structured messages. Adapting this coordination scheme allows robust hierarchical collaboration.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the coordination logic. Translate the message JSON format to target the Antigravity send_message tool parameters, ensuring the recipient and message contents align with the team configuration.
